_note: Self-study practice for Python Strings & Arithmetic. Inspired by BSCS 1B coursework — not an official platform.
title: LT Review

problems.title: Text Inspector
problems.description:
You are building a simple text inspection tool. A user types in a line of text and your program reports several facts about it using **string methods and slicing**.

Your program should:
1. Read a **single line of text** from the user.
2. Print the **total number of characters** (including spaces) using `len()`.
3. Print the text converted to **uppercase** using `upper()`.
4. Print the text converted to **lowercase** using `lower()`.
5. Print the text **reversed** using slicing.
constraints: The input can contain letters, digits, and spaces.
The input will be at least 1 character long.
problems.inputFormat: A single line of text (may contain letters, numbers, and spaces).
problems.outputFormat:
Line 1: `Length: <n>` — total number of characters.
Line 2: `Upper: <text>` — the text in all uppercase.
Line 3: `Lower: <text>` — the text in all lowercase.
Line 4: `Reversed: <text>` — the text reversed.
problems.hint:
text = input()
print('Length:', len(text))
print('Upper:', text.upper())
print('Lower:', text.lower())
print('Reversed:', text[::-1])

problems.title: Number Statistics Calculator
problems.description:
You are writing a batch statistics tool. A user provides a count and then a series of numbers, and your program calculates key statistics using Python's **built-in functions**.

Your program should:
1. Read an integer **n** — the count of numbers to process.
2. Read **n integers** separated by spaces on the next line.
3. Calculate and display the **sum**, **minimum**, **maximum**, and **average** (rounded to 2 decimal places).
constraints: Average is rounded to 2 decimal places.
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: An integer `n` — how many numbers to read.
Line 2: `n` integers separated by spaces.
problems.hint:
n = int(input())
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
print('Sum:', sum(nums))
print('Min:', min(nums))
print('Max:', max(nums))
print('Average:', round(sum(nums)/len(nums), 2))

testCases.label: Hidden: Mix of negatives and positives

problems.title: Power and Remainder Calculator
problems.description:
You are building an arithmetic calculator that performs **power and division operations** on two numbers. The program must also gracefully handle division by zero.

Your program should:
1. Read two integers **a** (base) and **b** (exponent/divisor) from a single line.
2. Calculate and display:
   - **a raised to the power of b** using `**`
   - **Floor division** of a by b using `//`
   - **Remainder** of a divided by b using `%`
   - **Quotient and remainder together** using `divmod()`
3. If **b is 0**, print `Error: Cannot divide by zero` for the last three operations (but still compute the power).
constraints: Both a and b are integers.
a can be any integer; b can be zero or positive.
If b is 0, only the power result is valid — display the error message for division operations.
problems.inputFormat: A single line containing two integers `a` and `b` separated by a space.
problems.outputFormat:
Line 1: `Power: <result>` — a raised to b.
Line 2: `Floor Division: <result>` — or `Error: Cannot divide by zero`.
Line 3: `Remainder: <result>` — or `Error: Cannot divide by zero`.
Line 4: `Divmod: <result>` — or `Error: Cannot divide by zero`.
problems.hint:
a, b = map(int, input().split())
print('Power:', a**b)
if b == 0:
    print('Floor Division: Error: Cannot divide by zero')
    print('Remainder: Error: Cannot divide by zero')
    print('Divmod: Error: Cannot divide by zero')
else:
    print('Floor Division:', a//b)
    print('Remainder:', a%b)
    print('Divmod:', divmod(a,b))

testCases.label: Example 3 — b is zero
expected: Floor Division: Error: Cannot divide by zero
Remainder: Error: Cannot divide by zero
Divmod: Error: Cannot divide by zero
